cards.grammarNote: Highly irregular verb. je vais, tu vas, il/elle va, nous allons, vous allez, ils/elles vont.
ça va = it goes. Used as both a question (How are you?) and an answer (Fine). One of the most common French expressions. Literally: 'it goes?'
allez-vous = subject-verb inversion for formal questions. Comment = how. The informal equivalent: Comment tu vas ? or just Ça va ?
aller + adverb of manner. je vais bien = I'm doing well. je vais mal = I'm not doing well. je vais mieux = I'm doing better.
mieux = better (irregular comparative of bien). je vais mieux is commonly used after being sick or sad.
ne...pas wraps aller: il ne va pas. The adverb bien comes after pas: ne va pas bien.
ça va aller = it will be okay / it'll work out. Uses the near future (aller + infinitive). A reassuring expression.
on y va = let's go / we're going (lit: one goes there). y is a pronoun replacing a place. Very common spoken expression.
aller + infinitive = near future (futur proche). je vais pleurer = I'm going to cry. Used for imminent or planned actions.
Informal version without inversion. Tu vas comment ? is also heard. In very casual speech: T'as le moral ?
An idiomatic expression: ça va sans dire = it goes without saying. Built from aller + sans + infinitive.
tout = everything/all. tout va bien is a reassuring statement. Tout va mal = everything is going wrong.
